exercises.title: Security Category Selection for Enterprise
exercises.description: Develop a security category selection policy for a fictional financial services company.
tasks: List 5 different data types the company handles (e.g., transactions, customer PII, internal docs)
Assign an appropriate security category to each data type
Justify each selection based on sensitivity and lifetime
Identify which PQC algorithms and parameter sets should be used
Document any regulatory considerations
exercises.expectedOutput: A security policy document with category assignments and justifications
hints: Long-term records may need higher categories than real-time data

exercises.title: Performance Impact Analysis
exercises.description: Calculate the performance and bandwidth impact of upgrading from Category 1 to Category 5.
tasks: Compare key sizes between ML-KEM-512 and ML-KEM-1024
Calculate the bandwidth increase for a TLS handshake
Estimate the CPU time difference for 1000 key exchanges
Determine storage requirements for 1 million certificates
Make recommendations for when Category 5 overhead is justified
exercises.expectedOutput: A quantitative analysis with specific numbers and recommendations
hints: TLS sends public key + ciphertext during handshake
Consider both network and computational overhead
Storage for certificates includes public key and signature

exercises.title: Security Margin Calculation
exercises.description: Research and explain the quantum security margins for each category.
tasks: Explain how Grover's algorithm affects each category
Calculate the effective quantum security bits for each category
Research the current state of quantum computer development
Estimate when each category might become vulnerable
Discuss the 'harvest now, decrypt later' threat
exercises.expectedOutput: A security analysis document with quantum threat timeline
hints: Grover's algorithm provides quadratic speedup for search
Consider both logical and physical qubit requirements
Current quantum computers have ~1000 physical qubits

exercises.title: Algorithm Selection Matrix
exercises.description: Create a decision matrix for selecting PQC algorithms and parameters.
tasks: Create a matrix with use cases as rows and algorithm options as columns
Include at least 8 different use cases
Rate each algorithm option for each use case (1-5 scale)
Provide selection criteria explanations
Identify cases where SLH-DSA is preferable despite larger signatures
exercises.expectedOutput: A comprehensive decision matrix with scoring and explanations
hints: Consider IoT, web servers, mobile apps, certificate authorities
Size constraints may eliminate some options for certain uses
